QF.id: Analysis of Equity and Debt Instruments_C1_1

question.text: Scenario: Meera Krishnan, a resident Indian aged 38, made several investment transactions during FY 2025-26. Her details are as follows: Transaction 1 - Listed Equity Shares of TechCorp Ltd: Purchased 2,000 shares at Rs. 450 per share on 15-Jun-2017. Fair Market Value (FMV) on 31-Jan-2018 was Rs. 520 per share. Sold all shares at Rs. 780 per share on 10-Oct-2025. STT paid on both purchase and sale. Transaction 2 - Unlisted Shares of StartUp Pvt Ltd: Purchased 5,000 shares at Rs. 30 per share on 01-Apr-2023. Sold all shares at Rs. 85 per share on 15-Dec-2025. Transaction 3 - Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGBs): Purchased 50 units at Rs. 4,800 per gram on 01-Mar-2020 (8-year maturity). Sold 20 units on NSE at Rs. 7,200 per gram on 20-Sep-2025. Interest received during FY 2025-26: Rs. 12,000. Transaction 4 - Listed Corporate Bonds: Purchased 500 bonds at Rs. 1,000 face value on 01-Jan-2025. Sold all at Rs. 1,050 on 15-Aug-2025. Annual coupon of 8% received. Transaction 5 - Bonus Shares of InfoSys Ltd: Meera held 1,000 shares of InfoSys purchased on 01-Mar-2022 at Rs. 1,500 per share. She received 1,000 bonus shares (1:1 ratio) on 15-Jun-2023. She sold all 1,000 bonus shares at Rs. 1,800 per share on 20-Jan-2026. STT paid. Meera's total salary income is Rs. 25,00,000 (30% tax bracket). She has no other income. Question 1 of 5: What is the Long-Term Capital Gain (LTCG) on the sale of TechCorp Ltd listed equity shares, and what is the tax payable on it under section 112A?

answer.text: LTCG is Rs. 5,20,000 and tax payable is Rs. 49,375
LTCG is Rs. 6,60,000 and tax payable is Rs. 66,875
LTCG is Rs. 5,20,000 and tax payable is Rs. 65,000
LTCG is Rs. 5,60,000 and tax payable is Rs. 54,375

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For listed equity shares purchased before 01-Feb-2018, the cost of acquisition under section 112A is computed as the HIGHER of (a) actual cost of acquisition (Rs. 450) or (b) the LOWER of FMV on 31-Jan-2018 (Rs. 520) and the actual sale price (Rs. 780). Lower of 520 and 780 = Rs. 520. Higher of 450 and 520 = Rs. 520. So cost of acquisition = Rs. 520. LTCG = (780 - 520) x 2,000 = Rs. 5,20,000. Holding period from Jun-2017 to Oct-2025 exceeds 12 months, so it is LTCG. Under section 112A, the first Rs. 1,25,000 of aggregate LTCG on listed equity is exempt. Tax = 12.5% x (5,20,000 - 1,25,000) = 12.5% x 3,95,000 = Rs. 49,375. **The Trap:** Rs. 6,60,000 uses the actual purchase cost of Rs. 450 instead of the grandfathered cost of Rs. 520 — LTCG = (780-450) x 2000 = 6,60,000. Rs. 65,000 applies 12.5% on the full Rs. 5,20,000 without deducting the Rs. 1,25,000 exemption. Rs. 5,60,000 incorrectly uses FMV of Rs. 500 (a rounded figure) instead of Rs. 520. **Cross-Exam Context:** The grandfathering provision under section 112A for shares acquired before 01-Feb-2018 is a frequently tested concept. Remember the formula: cost = higher of [actual cost] or [lower of (FMV on 31-Jan-2018, sale price)]. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always verify the FMV on 31-Jan-2018 from BSE/NSE historical data. Many investors forget the grandfathering benefit and over-report capital gains. This provision ensures gains accrued before 01-Feb-2018 are protected from the LTCG tax introduced in Budget 2018.
